title: Co-evolution of self-replication and function in a digital primordial soup
excerpt: While traditional evolutionary algorithms hard-code reproduction, self-replication can emerge spontaneously within digital ``primordial soups''. This paper investigates the co-evolution of this emergent self-replication alongside problem-solving capabilities. We initialize a population of random 32-byte Z80 assembly programs, requiring self-replication to arise purely through random assembly-level mutations and pairwise program interactions. To link these behaviors, we introduce a task-based validation step: correctly evaluating a polynomial raises a program's interaction probability above a baseline rate. Our experiments yield four primary findings. First, self-replication and mathematical problem-solving successfully co-evolve from initial randomness. Second, the pressure to compute accelerates the emergence of compact, robust reproductive architectures that preserve memory for task execution. Third, applying metabolic constraints increases the likelihood that programs evolve conditional halting, terminating early during validation while bypassing the halt during interaction to execute block-copy replication. Finally, when programs are partitioned into spatial task niches, spontaneous self-replication generates an emergent learning curriculum, utilizing simple solutions as stepping stones toward complex polynomials. Altogether, these results demonstrate an interactive feedback loop: environmental task demands actively shape the physical architecture of self-replication, while spontaneous replication alters the evolutionary trajectory of functional problem-solving.
content_html:
Authors:Francesco Cicala, Eyvind Niklasson, Ettore Randazzo, Sami Boukortt, Alessio Basti, Mayalen Etcheverry, Rif A. Saurous, Ben Laurie, James Manyika, Blaise Aguera-Arcas, Blake Richards

View PDF HTML (experimental)

Abstract:While traditional evolutionary algorithms hard-code reproduction, self-replication can emerge spontaneously within digital ``primordial soups''. This paper investigates the co-evolution of this emergent self-replication alongside problem-solving capabilities. We initialize a population of random 32-byte Z80 assembly programs, requiring self-replication to arise purely through random assembly-level mutations and pairwise program interactions. To link these behaviors, we introduce a task-based validation step: correctly evaluating a polynomial raises a program's interaction probability above a baseline rate. Our experiments yield four primary findings. First, self-replication and mathematical problem-solving successfully co-evolve from initial randomness. Second, the pressure to compute accelerates the emergence of compact, robust reproductive architectures that preserve memory for task execution. Third, applying metabolic constraints increases the likelihood that programs evolve conditional halting, terminating early during validation while bypassing the halt during interaction to execute block-copy replication. Finally, when programs are partitioned into spatial task niches, spontaneous self-replication generates an emergent learning curriculum, utilizing simple solutions as stepping stones toward complex polynomials. Altogether, these results demonstrate an interactive feedback loop: environmental task demands actively shape the physical architecture of self-replication, while spontaneous replication alters the evolutionary trajectory of functional problem-solving.
Subjects: Neural and Evolutionary Computing (cs.NE)

blocks.html_en: Abstract:While traditional evolutionary algorithms hard-code reproduction, self-replication can emerge spontaneously within digital ``primordial soups''. This paper investigates the co-evolution of this emergent self-replication alongside problem-solving capabilities. We initialize a population of random 32-byte Z80 assembly programs, requiring self-replication to arise purely through random assembly-level mutations and pairwise program interactions. To link these behaviors, we introduce a task-based validation step: correctly evaluating a polynomial raises a program's interaction probability above a baseline rate. Our experiments yield four primary findings. First, self-replication and mathematical problem-solving successfully co-evolve from initial randomness. Second, the pressure to compute accelerates the emergence of compact, robust reproductive architectures that preserve memory for task execution. Third, applying metabolic constraints increases the likelihood that programs evolve conditional halting, terminating early during validation while bypassing the halt during interaction to execute block-copy replication. Finally, when programs are partitioned into spatial task niches, spontaneous self-replication generates an emergent learning curriculum, utilizing simple solutions as stepping stones toward complex polynomials. Altogether, these results demonstrate an interactive feedback loop: environmental task demands actively shape the physical architecture of self-replication, while spontaneous replication alters the evolutionary trajectory of functional problem-solving.

sentences.en: Abstract:While traditional evolutionary algorithms hard-code reproduction, self-replication can emerge spontaneously within digital ``primordial soups''.
sentences.zh: 摘要：虽然传统的进化算法硬编码复制，但自我复制可以在数字“原始汤”中自发出现。

sentences.en: This paper investigates the co-evolution of this emergent self-replication alongside problem-solving capabilities.
sentences.zh: 本文研究了这种新兴的自我复制与解决问题的能力的共同进化。

sentences.en: We initialize a population of random 32-byte Z80 assembly programs, requiring self-replication to arise purely through random assembly-level mutations and pairwise program interactions.
sentences.zh: 我们初始化了一组随机 32 字节 Z80 汇编程序，要求纯粹通过随机汇编级突变和成对程序交互来实现自我复制。

sentences.en: To link these behaviors, we introduce a task-based validation step: correctly evaluating a polynomial raises a program's interaction probability above a baseline rate.
sentences.zh: 为了将这些行为联系起来，我们引入了一个基于任务的验证步骤：正确评估多项式可以将程序的交互概率提高到基线率以上。

sentences.en: Our experiments yield four primary findings.
sentences.zh: 我们的实验产生了四个主要发现。

sentences.en: First, self-replication and mathematical problem-solving successfully co-evolve from initial randomness.
sentences.zh: 首先，自我复制和数学问题解决从最初的随机性成功地共同进化。

sentences.en: Second, the pressure to compute accelerates the emergence of compact, robust reproductive architectures that preserve memory for task execution.
sentences.zh: 其次，计算压力加速了紧凑、强大的复制架构的出现，这些架构为任务执行保留了内存。

sentences.en: Third, applying metabolic constraints increases the likelihood that programs evolve conditional halting, terminating early during validation while bypassing the halt during interaction to execute block-copy replication.
sentences.zh: 第三，应用代谢约束增加了程序发生条件停止的可能性，即在验证期间提前终止，同时在交互期间绕过停止以执行块复制复制。

sentences.en: Finally, when programs are partitioned into spatial task niches, spontaneous self-replication generates an emergent learning curriculum, utilizing simple solutions as stepping stones toward complex polynomials.
sentences.zh: 最后，当程序被划分为空间任务领域时，自发的自我复制会产生一个新兴的学习课程，利用简单的解决方案作为通向复杂多项式的垫脚石。

sentences.en: Altogether, these results demonstrate an interactive feedback loop: environmental task demands actively shape the physical architecture of self-replication, while spontaneous replication alters the evolutionary trajectory of functional problem-solving.
sentences.zh: 总而言之，这些结果展示了一个交互式反馈循环：环境任务需求主动塑造自我复制的物理架构，而自发复制改变了功能性问题解决的进化轨迹。

content_html_zh:
作者：Francesco Cicala、Eyvind Niklasson、Ettore Randazzo、Sami Boukortt、Alessio Basti、Mayalen Etcheverry、Rif A. 萨罗斯、本·劳瑞、詹姆斯·马尼卡、布莱斯·阿格拉-阿卡斯、布莱克·理查兹

查看 PDF HTML（实验性）

摘要：虽然传统的进化算法硬编码复制，但自我复制可以在数字“原始汤”中自发出现。 本文研究了这种新兴的自我复制与解决问题的能力的共同进化。 我们初始化了一组随机 32 字节 Z80 汇编程序，要求纯粹通过随机汇编级突变和成对程序交互来实现自我复制。 为了将这些行为联系起来，我们引入了一个基于任务的验证步骤：正确评估多项式可以将程序的交互概率提高到基线率以上。 我们的实验产生了四个主要发现。 首先，自我复制和数学问题解决从最初的随机性成功地共同进化。 其次，计算压力加速了紧凑、强大的复制架构的出现，这些架构为任务执行保留了内存。 第三，应用代谢约束增加了程序发生条件停止的可能性，即在验证期间提前终止，同时在交互期间绕过停止以执行块复制复制。 最后，当程序被划分为空间任务领域时，自发的自我复制会产生一个新兴的学习课程，利用简单的解决方案作为通向复杂多项式的垫脚石。 总而言之，这些结果展示了一个交互式反馈循环：环境任务需求主动塑造自我复制的物理架构，而自发复制改变了功能性问题解决的进化轨迹。
科目： 神经和进化计算 (cs.NE)
引用为： arXiv:2607.09211 [cs.NE]
  （或此版本的 arXiv:2607.09211v1 [cs.NE]）
